content:
How I used Python and AI to automate image quality control, sort thousands of photos, and save hours of manual work.

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Traditional wooden fishing boats resting on a muddy riverbank with green grass during low tide, part of a Bangladesh coastal and river landscape under a cloudy sky. image captured by authorOver the past 10 years, as a passionate photographer, I have traveled extensively both at home and abroad. Wherever I go, my beloved camera is always with me. Nature, people’s daily lives, the streets — whenever something catches my eye, I try to frame it.
Taking photos during a trip is absolutely amazing! But the real nightmare begins when I return home and transfer those photos to my computer. Before I know it, my computer’s storage is completely full.
Those of us who do photography know very well that to get one perfect shot, we click the exact same scene over and over again, from different angles, in different moods, and with different settings. Trying to capture that one great shot might result in twenty to thirty pictures of the same subject. But after the trip, manually picking out the best one from these thousands of photos, one by one, is incredibly frustrating and time-consuming — only those who have suffered through it can truly understand. Staring at the screen for hours on end just makes your eyes ache.
